free_text: reactions in thin and thick Pb and U targets from
Cylindrical Pb target of 0.2-36 cm thickness with
diameters of 8,12 and 15 cm. Depleted U (0.2% U-235,
density 18.95 g/cm**2) by stacking 3 mm thick and
5*5 cm**2 U-metal sheets mounted in a frame of 7*7
cm**2 glass fiber. The front and back side of these U
slices were covered by 0.1-mm thin mylar foils. The 20-
and 40-cm long U-targets had a diameter of 8-cm. The
longest target of 40-cm that fits in neutron detector
corresponds  to about 2.1 or 3.5 reaction lengths
for Pb and U, respectively, that is about 88% or 97% of
the incident protons have undergone at least one
reaction in Pb or U. Since will compare below mean
neutron multiplicities for a 40 cm long U and a 35 cm
long Pb target it should be point out here that a 40 cm
U target contains 66% more nuclei/unit area than a
The lead targets were produced from standard lead
bricks used for shielding, which are contaminated with
antimony (2% for the employed lead) having a high
integrated neutron capture cross section of 170 barns
compared to 0.137 barns for pure lead.  Thus, some of
the neutrons which were thermalized in the lead target
or are coming back from the surrounding scintillator
liquid of the employed detector might be lost.
consists of a spherical shell with outer and inner
diameters of 140 and 40 cm, respectively.  This 50 cm
thick shell is filled with 1500 litres of scintillator
liquid loaded with gadolinium, 0.4% by weight.
Some scintillation detectors to improve neutron
detector characteristics.  See details in reference
given below have been corrected for background and dead
Not corrected but not for detection efficiency, since
such a correction would require the knowledge of the
energy of each detected neutron, which is
experimentally not measured. The mean neutron
multiplicities as well as the most probable neutron
multiplicity have been corrected in addition for an
assumed average neutron detection efficiency of 85% as
REACTION code in Subents 2-17 according MEMO CP-C/406.
